title: tanacetum argenteum var. pumilum
intro: Tanacetum argenteum var. pumilum, also known as dwarf tansy or low-growing tansy, is a compact, shrubby perennial plant that belongs to the sunflower family. Traditionally, the plant's flowers, leaves, and roots have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting digestive health, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids. In herbal preparations, Tanacetum argenteum var. pumilum is often used as a tincture, infusion, or dried herb in teas, capsules, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
